nodes.itemHTML: • Lua-users style guide - A general, high-level style guide; unopinionated, easily agreed on.
• Olivine style guidestars375 - A more opinionated and specific, and therefore more rigorous, guide.
• inspect.luastars888 - Human-readable representation of Lua tables.
• Serstars66 - Dead simple serializer with good performance.
• vstreamstars56 - Instrumentable streams mix-ins to inspect a pipeline of streams.
• Metaluastars304 - Pure Lua parser and compiler, used for generating ASTs. A number of other tools make use of the Metalua parser in this way.
• kpiestars68 - A scripting utility to juggle windows.

• Lua - Lua's original ANSI C interpreter.
    • Lua Repostars4.3k - The official Lua repo, as seen by the Lua team, mirrored to GitHub.
• LuaJIT - High-performance Just-In-Time compiler for Lua.
• Moonshinestars472 - A Lua VM implemented in JavaScript. Slower than lua.vm.js, but with better docs, examples, and JS interfacing.
• ProFi - Simple profiler that works with LuaJIT and produces a report file.
• luatracestars147 - Toolset for tracing/analyzing/profiling script execution and generating detailed reports.
• StackTracePlusstars171 - Drop-in upgrade to Lua's stack traces which adds local context and improves readability.
• MobDebugstars670 - Powerful remote debugger with breakpoints and stack inspection. Used by ZeroBraneStudio.
• Lua for IDEA - IntelliJ IDEA plugin which, among other things, provides code completion, smart highlighting, and experimental debugging.
• Mosesstars547 - Functional programming utility belt, inspired by Underscore.js.
• Penlightstars1.3k - Broad, heavyweight utility library, inspired by Python's standard libs. Provides the batteries that Lua doesn't.
• lua-stdlibstars229 - Middle-weight standard library extension; adds some useful data structures, utility functions, and basic functional stuff.
• Microlightstars141 - A little library of useful Lua functions; the 'extra light' version of Penlight.
• LÖVE 2D - Desktop game development platform. Cross-platform, feature-complete, well-adopted.
• Jumperstars544 - Fast, lightweight, and easy-to-use pathfinding library for grid-based games.
• lumestars574 - Utility belt library geared toward game development.
• NoobHubstars260 - Network multiplayer for Corona, LÖVE, and more, following a simple pub-sub model.

• Collision detection
    • bump.luastars667 - Minimal rectangle-based collision detection which handles tunnelling and basic collision resolution.
    • HardonCollider - Detect collisions between arbitrarily positioned and rotated shapes of any type.

• Core platform
    • ngx_lua - The core piece of OpenResty. Embeds Lua in Nginx and exposes, among other things, the cosocket API for non-blocking sockets (compatible with LuaSocket's API).
    • OpenResty GitHub Organization - Home of the repositories for ngx_lua, ngx_openresty, and many related modules.

• Third-party modules
    • lua-resty-httpstars1.5k - Lua HTTP client driver, built on the cosocket API.

• Frameworks & tools
    • Lapis - Full-stack framework for Lua and OpenResty. Like the Django or Rails of Lua. Supports Moonscript.
    • ledgestars419 - Lua module providing scriptable, RFC-compliant HTTP cache functionality.
    • Sailorstars872 — An MVC web framework compatible with OpenResty, Apache and other webservers.
    • Kongstars28.9k - Microservice & API Management Layer.
• ansicolorsstars86 - Simple function for printing to the console in color.
• cliargsstars85 - A simple command-line argument parsing module.
• lua-termstars97 - Terminal operations and manipulations.

• Coroutine-based multitasking:
    • Lumenstars127 - Simple concurrent task scheduling.
    • ConcurrentLuastars155 - Implements an Erlang-style message-passing concurrency model.
    • cqueues - Library for managing sockets, signals, and threads based on an event loop with coroutines.

• Multithreading:
    • llthreadsstars137 - A simple wrapper for low-level pthreads & WIN32 threads.
    • llthreads2stars67 - Newer rewrite of llthreads.
    • lanesstars334 - Library implementing a message passing model with one OS thread per Lua thread.
    • luaprocstars99 - Message-passing model which allows multiple threads per OS thread and easily generalizes across a network. See also the paper where it originated.
• lustache - Mustache template implementation.
• etluastars167 - Embedded Lua templates, ERB-style.
• lua-resty-templatestars789 - Lua-oriented template engine for OpenResty, somewhat Jinja-like.
• LDoc - Documentation generator which modernizes and extends LuaDoc.
• 30logstars316 - Minimalist OOP library with basic classes, inheritance, and mixins in 30 lines.
• middleclassstars1.3k - Simple but robust OOP library with inheritance, methods, metamethods, class variables and mixins.
• luaposixstars399 - Bindings for POSIX APIs, including curses.
• LuaDatestars191 - Date and time module with parsing, formatting, addition/subtraction, localization, and ISO 8601 support.
• cron.luastars128 - Time-related functions for Lua, inspired by JavaScript's setTimeout and setInterval.
• magickstars335 - Lua bindings to ImageMagick for LuaJIT using FFI.
• Torch7 - Scientific computing framework with wide support for machine learning algorithms, used by Facebook, Google, and more.

• JSON
    • lua-cjsonstars726 - Blazing fast JSON encoding/decoding implemented in C and exposed to Lua.
    • luajsonstars235 - JSON encoder/decoder implemented in Lua on top of LPeg.
    • dkjson - JSON encoder/decoder implemented in pure Lua.
    • json.luastars1k - A fast and tiny JSON library in pure Lua.

• XML
    • LuaExpat - SAX XML parser via binding to the Expat library.
    • SLAXMLstars133 - Pure Lua SAX-like streaming XML parser.
• lunamarkstars149 - Converts Markdown to other textual formats including HTML and LaTeX. Uses LPeg for fast parsing.
• i18n.luastars182 - Internationalization library with locales, formatting, and pluralization.
• serpentstars394 - Serializer and pretty printer.
• lua-zlibstars206 - Simple streaming interface to zlib for gzip/gunzip.
• lua-zipstars63 - Lua binding to libzip. Reads and writes zip files.
• lua-resty-mysqlstars611 - Lua MySQL driver for OpenResty.
• busted - BDD-style unit testing framework with great docs and Moonscript support.
• telescopestars142 - Flexible and highly customizable testing library.
• luassertstars103 - Assertion library extending Lua's built-in assertions.
• LuaJIT FFI - LuaJIT's mechanism for calling external C functions and using C data structures from pure Lua code.
• luaffistars397 - Standalone FFI library, compatible with the LuaJIT FFI interface.

• luacov - Simple coverage analyzer, used by busted and telescope for checking test coverage.
    • luacov-coverallsstars43 - LuaCov reporter for coveralls.io.
• luacheckstars1.4k - Simple static analyzer which detects accidental globals and undefined or shadowed locals.
• LuaInspectstars153 - Lua's most powerful code analysis and linting tool, built on Metalua. Used by ZeroBraneStudio, among others.
• LuaMinifystars198 - Minifier which also brings its own static analysis tools, lexer, and parser.
• punchdrunk.jsstars78 - Moonshine + LÖVE API reimplementation = run LÖVE games in the browser.
• luvitstars3.3k - Node.js's underlying architecture (libUV) with Lua on top instead of JavaScript.

• MoonScript - Moonscript is a dynamic scripting language that compiles to Lua. It reduces verbosity and provides a rich set of features like comprehensions and classes. Its author calls it 'CoffeeScript for Lua'.
• sitegen - A static site generator which uses MoonScript and supports HTML and Markdown, page grouping, and plugins.
• lua-users wiki - A large community-maintained collection of Lua information and resources, supplementing the official website.
• Lua Crash Course - Short crash course readover, or reference for when you forget the basics.
• Learn Lua in 15 Minutes - A well-commented example file which covers the basics.
• Learning Lua from JS - An overview of the similarities and differences between Lua and JS; a great start for JavaScript folks looking to pick up Lua.
• lua-users tutorial - In-depth collection of tutorials aimed at newcomers.
• Lua Missionsstars284 - A series of 'Missions' to work through which are designed to teach aspects of Lua along the way.
• Creating an Image Server - Walks through setting up and using OpenResty to build a simple image processing server; a great starting point for playing with OpenResty.
• Lua: Good, bad, and ugly parts - A thorough summary of the good, different, bad, and ugly aspects of Lua, including many subtle quirks, by the author of ZeroBraneStudio.
• Roberto's Talks - History of talks given by Lua's chief architect, with slides for each.
• Lua Workshop Talks - High-quality talks are given at each ~annual Lua Workshop, and a history of them is online, slides included.
• Programming in Lua - The authoritative intro to all aspects of Lua programming, written by Lua's chief architect. Three editions released; first edition available online.
• Programming Gems - A collection of articles covering existing wisdom and practices on programming well in Lua, in a broad variety of use cases.
• DC.js - Dimensional charting built to work natively with crossfilter rendered using d3.js. Excellent for connecting charts/additional metadata to hover events in D3.
• IPython - provides a rich architecture for interactive computing.
• 2014 - Stanford - Mining of Massive Datasets.
• es6ifystars601 - Traceur compiler wrapped as a Browserify v2 transform
• webassemblystars69 - Web DSL for Elixir.
• Mathias Bynens: JavaScript ♥ Unicode [26:12]
• Red Gate Books
• TONC GBA Programming - Game Boy Advance Development
• Software Foundations
• Building Java Projects with Gradle
• Visual Studio .NET Tips and Tricks (VS 2003-2005 only)
• Template Toolkit Documentation
• GNU Prolog Manual (PDF)
• Ruby on Rails Guides
• Introducing Microsoft SQL Server 2014
• libde265stars1.1k - Open h.265 video codec implementation. [LGPL] website
• OpenH264stars4.2k - Open Source H.264 Codec. [BSD] website
• Vorbis - Ogg Vorbis is a fully open, non-proprietary, patent-and-royalty-free, general-purpose compressed audio format. [BSD]
• Opus - A totally open, royalty-free, highly versatile audio codec. [BSD]
• Speex - A free codec for free speech. Obsoleted by Opus. [BSD]
• JUCEstars2.9k - An all-encompassing C++ class library for developing cross-platform software. [Core-Module: ISC, Rest: GPL2/GPL3/Commercial] website
• PhysicsFS - A library to provide abstract access to various archives. It is intended for use in video games, and the design was somewhat inspired by Quake 3's file subsystem. [zlib]
• C++Reactstars902 - A reactive programming library for C++11. [Boost]
• QtAvstars2.8k - A multimedia playback framework based on Qt and FFmpeg to write a player easily. [LGPL] website
• QDjangostars219 - A web framework written in C++ and built on top of the Qt library. Where possible it tries to follow django's API, hence its name. [LGPL]
• FMOD - An easy to use crossplatform audio engine and audio content creation tool for games. [Free for non-commercial/Commercial]
• Maximilianstars1.2k - C++ Audio and Music DSP Library. [MIT]
• OpenAL - Open Audio Library - A crossplatform audio API. [BSD/LGPL/Commercial]
• Tonicstars424 - Easy and efficient audio synthesis in C++. [Unlicense]
• bzip2 - A freely available, patent free, high-quality data compressor. [BSD]
• LZHAM - Lossless data compression library with a compression ratio similar to LZMA but with much faster decompression. [BSD]
• LZMAT - An extremely fast real-time lossless data compression library. [GPL]
• Minizipstars767 - Zlib with latest bug fixes that supports PKWARE disk spanning, AES encryption, and IO buffering. [zlib]
• ZLib - A very compact compression library for data streams. [zlib]
• ZZIPlib - Provides read access on ZIP-archives. [MPL/LGPL]
• Eigen - A high-level C++ library of template headers for linear algebra, matrix and vector operations, numerical solvers and related algorithms. [MPL2]
• libsodiumstars9.2k - P(ortable|ackageable) NaCl-based crypto library, opinionated and easy to use. [ISC]
• ISO C++ Standards Committee - ISO/IEC JTC1/SC22/WG21 - The C++ Standards Committee. website
• Clockworkstars3.5k - Integrates Clockwork Chrome extension for debugging and profiling apps
• btskstars357 - Game Behavior Tree Starter Kit. [zlib]
• Evolving Objects - A template-based, ANSI-C++ evolutionary computation library which helps you to write your own stochastic optimization algorithms insanely fast. [LGPL]
• Boost.Asio - A cross-platform C++ library for network and low-level I/O programming. [Boost]
• libev - A full-featured and high-performance event loop that is loosely modelled after libevent, but without its limitations and bugs. [BSD and GPL]
• libevent - An event notification library. [BSD]
• ASL - Adobe Source Libraries provides peer-reviewed and portable C++ source libraries. [MIT]
• ffead-cppstars447 - Framework for Enterprise Application Development. [Apache2]
• Follystars16.8k - An open-source C++ library developed and used at Facebook. [Apache2]
• libPhenomstars1.7k - libPhenom is an eventing framework for building high performance and high scalability systems in C. [Apache2]
• LibSourceystars1.2k - C++11 evented IO for real-time video streaming and high performance networking applications. [LGPL]
• LibUstars328 - A multiplatform utility library written in C. [BSD]
• Loki - A C++ library of designs, containing flexible implementations of common design patterns and idioms. [MIT]
• STXXL - Standard Template Library for Extra Large Data Sets. [Boost]
• Ultimate++ - A C++ cross-platform rapid application development framework. [BSD]
• Windows Template Library - A C++ library for developing Windows applications and UI components. [Public]
• merchantstars967 - A Django app to accept payments from various payment processors.

• Code Coverage
    • coverage - Code coverage measurement.
• CppCMS - A Free High Performance Web Development Framework (not a CMS). [LGPLv3]
• Crowstars6.3k - Crow is C++ micro web framework (inspired by Python Flask). [BSD]
• Kore - ultra fast and flexible web server / framework for web applications developed in C. [ISC]
• libOnion - lightweight library to help you create webservers in C programming language. [LGPLv3]
• Wt - A C++ library for developing web applications. [GPL/Commercial]
• cerealstars2.8k - A C++11 library for serialization. [BSD]
• SimpleBinaryEncodingstars2.4k - encoding and decoding application messages in binary format for low-latency applications. [Apache2]
• Tenantistars532 - Multi-tenant database schema manager
• internstars4.3k - A next-generation code testing stack for JavaScript.
• Entruststars6.2k - Role-based Permissions
• Laroutestars774 - Generate Laravel route URLs from JavaScript
• Envoystars1.4k - SSH Task Runner
• gopencilsstars438 - Small and simple package to easily consume REST APIs.
• erl-rstarstars54 - An Erlang implementation of the R*-tree spacial data structure.
• GeoCouchstars494 - A spatial extension for Couchbase and Apache CouchDB.
• Telesstars14 - An Erlang network service for manipulating geographic data.
• Kefirstars7 - FRP library for JavaScript inspired by Bacon.js and RxJS with focus on high performance and low memory consumption.

• Open Source Projects:
    • Cumulusstars44 – Hacker news like website with the OCaml framework Ocsigen
